title: Registry Identity, SBOM, Provenance, Attestations, and Promotion · Stakeholder Delivery Mission
summary: A health-information ingestion team. The team needs a fork-safe, digest-bound Buildx release with platform tests, scoped cache, SBOM, provenance, attestation verification, and a defended SLSA claim. Planned artifact: Deliver Attested Multi-Platform Container Release with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin third-party actions to reviewed full commit SHAs, retain readable version comments, and automate reviewed update proposals.
Use secret mounts, scoped cache backends, deterministic keys, trusted writers, and no credential-bearing ARG, layer, log, or attestation.
Build declared platforms with Buildx, distinguish native and emulated execution, test each output, and verify the manifest index and platform digests.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Attestation Verification Policy
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-attestation-verification-policy-wygi1t". Tailor its attested-release.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Attestation Verification Policy.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Attestation Verification Policy into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Project Ci Attestation Verification Policy, keep Ci Attestation Verification Policy connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-attestation-verification-policy-wygi1t
  - competency: ci-attestation-verification-policy
    artifact: attested-release.yaml
    decision: attestation verification policy
    environment: single-job ephemeral self-hosted runner in a segmented subnet
    fault-injection: remove one supported Node and operating-system matrix cell
    retained-proof: job result dependency graph exact command and first causal failure
    case-key: wygi1t
    change: |
      subject: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
      tags: [v2.4.0, stable]
      sbom: spdx-attached
      provenance: mode-max
      builder: github-hosted-reviewed-workflow
      actions-attest: 4
      attestation-subject: exact-digest
      verification: issuer-repository-workflow-builder-subject
      promotion: same-digest
      rollback: prior-verified-digest
    verify: verify image digest SBOM provenance signature issuer repository workflow builder subject predicate policy and promotion identity
    failure: Reject tags as identity, detached unbound SBOM, build-arg secrets, signature-only trust, unverified attestations, or transitive SLSA claims.
    repair: Bind metadata to digest, generate safe attestations, verify identity and expectations downstream, promote once, and state SLSA limits.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: The Ci Attestation Verification Policy structural check is only one gate. attested image release record must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify signature, issuer, subject, source repository, workflow identity, builder, digest, predicate, and policy before promotion or deployment.

hints: For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Ci Attestation Verification Policy, name the event, revision, actor, permission, runner, dependency, artifact, environment, deployment, and recovery boundary, Ci Attestation Verification Policy precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Ci Attestation Verification Policy, trace this concrete input and predict evaluation, shell, job graph, cache, artifact, trust, deployment, signal, rollback, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A project candidate is promoted by mutable tag with partial SBOM and provenance, an unverified attestation, and policy exception 6. Bind image index, platforms, signatures, attestations, verification policy, and exception expiry. Competency probe: Verify signature, issuer, subject, source repository, workflow identity, builder, digest, predicate, and policy before promotion or deployment.

For Defend the stakeholder delivery · Project Ci Attestation Verification Policy, challenge the documented misconception with workflow, run, artifact, policy, fault, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A valid signature is sufficient even when its identity and subject are unexpected.
